title: 10 medicinal constituents of Calea ternifolia (jimsonweed)

constituents_list.constituent_desc: Calea ternifolia ternofoline is a unique alkaloid compound extracted from the plant. It has been traditionally used in Mexican and Latin American folk medicine for its sedative and anxiolytic properties. Ternofoline is believed to have a calming effect on the nervous system, reducing anxiety and stress levels. Its potential benefits also extend to treating insomnia, epilepsy, and other neurological disorders.

Calea ternifolia isoternofoline is a significant compound found in this plant species. Isoternofoline has been studied for its potential pharmacological activities, including antimicrobial, antioxidant, and anti-inflammatory properties. Its chemical structure consists of a unique combination of indole and quinoline rings, which may contribute to its therapeutic effects. Further research is needed to fully understand the bioactivities and potential medicinal applications of isoternofoline.

Calea ternifolia zaluzanin is a perennial herb native to Mexico and Central America. Its leaves are pinnatifid with 3-5 lobes, while its flowers are bright yellow, daisy-like, and have a distinctive sweet fragrance. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for centuries to treat various ailments, including insomnia, anxiety, and depression, as well as being used as an insect repellent.

Calea ternifolia ternofolinone is a type of alkaloid compound extracted from the plant's leaves and stems. This unique compound has been traditionally used in Mexican herbal medicine for centuries to induce vivid dreams, improve sleep quality, and treat anxiety and depression. Ternofolinone is believed to interact with the brain's neurotransmitters, promoting relaxation and reducing stress levels.

Calea ternifolia zaluzaninin is a sesquiterpene lactone found in the leaves of this plant. It has been traditionally used in Mexico and Central America for its sedative and hypnotic properties, often consumed as an infusion or tea to promote relaxation and reduce anxiety. Zaluzaninin's mechanisms of action are not fully understood, but it is believed to interact with GABA receptors in the brain, contributing to its calming effects.

Calea ternifolia caleic acid is a bioactive compound found in the plant's leaves. It has been traditionally used in Mexican folk medicine to treat various ailments, including insomnia, anxiety, and respiratory issues. Caleic acid has been shown to possess sedative and anxiolytic properties, making it a potential natural remedy for stress-related disorders.

Calea ternifolia furofuron lignans is a class of chemical compounds found in its leaves. These lignans have been shown to possess a range of bioactive properties, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. They have been traditionally used in Mexican folk medicine to treat various ailments, such as fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues. Research has also identified potential therapeutic applications for these compounds in the treatment of cancer and neurodegenerative diseases.

Calea ternifolia sesquiterpenoids is a class of bioactive compounds extracted from its leaves and flowers. These sesquiterpenoids have been studied for their potential pharmacological activities, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. They have also shown promise in the treatment of various diseases such as Alzheimer's disease, cancer, and cardiovascular disorders, making them a valuable area of research in the field of natural products chemistry.

Calea ternifolia flavonoids is a class of bioactive compounds found in its leaves and stems. These flavonoids have been studied for their potential health benefits, including antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. They may help protect against oxidative stress, improve cardiovascular health, and even possess antimicrobial and antifungal activities. Additionally, flavonoids from Calea ternifolia have shown promise in traditional medicine for treating various ailments, such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues.

Calea ternifolia tannins is a type of compound found in its leaves. Tannins are a group of polyphenolic compounds that give the plant its characteristic bitter taste and astringent properties. In traditional medicine, Calea ternifolia tannins have been used to treat various ailments such as fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues due to their antimicrobial and antiseptic properties.

intro: Calea ternifolia has active constituents such as terpenoids, alkaloids, and glycosides. These compounds possess sedative, antispasmodic, and anti-inflammatory properties, making it a popular herbal remedy for anxiety, insomnia, and stress relief. The constituents can help improve sleep quality, reducing symptoms of depression and anxiety disorders. Additionally, the plant's anti-inflammatory properties may aid in pain management and wound healing, potentially improving overall health and wellbeing.
